description: Fix WebSocket resource leaks in E2E tests where ws.close() is not wrapped in try/finally blocks. If an assertion fails between WebSocket open and close, the connection leaks. The malformed messages test in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts already uses the correct pattern — apply it consistently across all WebSocket-creating tests.

userStories.title: Wrap WebSocket cleanup in try/finally in lifecycle.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want WebSocket connections in lifecycle E2E tests to be cleaned up even when assertions fail, so that orphaned connections don't accumulate on test failure.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 WebSocket-creating tests in lifecycle.e2e.ts wrap ws.close() in a finally block
The WebSocket connect await remains outside the try block (only assertions and post-connect logic are inside try)
All existing assertions and test logic are preserved — no behavioral changes

userStories.title: Wrap WebSocket cleanup in try/finally in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want fake WebSocket connections in dispatch resilience E2E tests to be cleaned up even when assertions fail, so that orphaned connections don't accumulate on test failure.
acceptanceCriteria: All 3 fake-WebSocket-creating tests in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts wrap fakeWs.close() in a finally block
The WebSocket connect await remains outside the try block
All existing assertions, cleanup steps (setSlow, waitForExtensionConnected), and test logic are preserved
